item.title: About
item.description:
Introduction

The Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) and its generalizations in the form of Tensor Network States have made significant contributions across a wide range of fields, including condensed matter physics, statistical physics, quantum chemistry, nuclear physics, quantum information, and machine learning.

The Tensor Network States: Algorithms and Applications (TNSAA) workshops were established to highlight these developments, providing a platform for presenting new algorithmic advances and their applications in uncovering novel physics.

item.title: Call for Contributions
item.description:
TNSAA 2026 welcomes contributed presentations from researchers working on tensor network states, algorithms, and applications, as well as related areas.

Contributions may be considered for either oral or poster presentations.

There will be no formal proceedings for TNSAA 2026. The purpose of the contributed presentations is to encourage discussion, exchange recent results, and foster collaborations within the tensor network and related research communities.

Topics

Relevant topics include, but are not limited to:

item.title: Contact
item.description:
For inquiries regarding TNSAA 2026, please contact:

TNSAA 2026 Organizing Committee

Email:
ueda.hiroshi.qiqb@osaka-u.ac.jp

Venue

RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS)
Kobe, Japan

item.title: News
item.description:
Registration and contribution submission are now open

Registration and contribution submission for TNSAA 2026 are now open.

The contribution submission deadline is October 15, 2026.
Notification of acceptance will be sent by October 31, 2026.
The registration deadline is November 15, 2026.

Please visit the Registration page or Call for Contributions page for details.

Website launched

The official website of TNSAA 2026 is now online.

Venue confirmed

TNSAA 2026 will be held at the RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS), Kobe, Japan.

item.title: Program
item.description:
The detailed program will be announced closer to the workshop dates.

The tentative program below is subject to change.

Tentative Program

The detailed program will be announced closer to the workshop dates. The schedule below is tentative and subject to change.

December 15 (Tue)

Time Program
12:30–13:00 Registration
13:00–13:15 Opening
13:15–13:45 Talk 1: TBA
13:45–14:15 Talk 2: TBA
14:15–14:45 Talk 3: TBA
14:45–15:15 Coffee Break
15:15–15:45 Talk 4: TBA
15:45–16:15 Talk 5: TBA
16:15–16:45 Talk 6: TBA
16:45–17:15 Discussion / Buffer

December 16 (Wed)

Time Program
09:00–09:30 Talk 7: TBA
09:30–10:00 Talk 8: TBA
10:00–10:30 Coffee Break
10:30–11:00 Talk 9: TBA
11:00–11:30 Talk 10: TBA
11:30–13:30 Lunch Break
13:30–14:00 Talk 11: TBA
14:00–14:30 Talk 12: TBA
14:30–15:00 Talk 13: TBA
15:00–15:30 Coffee Break
15:30–17:00 Poster Session
18:00–20:00 Banquet / Networking Reception

December 17 (Thu)

Time Program
09:00–09:30 Talk 14: TBA
09:30–10:00 Talk 15: TBA
10:00–10:30 Coffee Break
10:30–11:00 Talk 16: TBA
11:00–11:30 Talk 17: TBA
11:30–13:30 Lunch Break
13:30–14:00 Talk 18: TBA
14:00–14:30 Talk 19: TBA
14:30–15:00 Coffee Break

December 18 (Fri)

Time Program
09:00–09:30 Talk 20: TBA
09:30–10:00 Talk 21: TBA
10:00–10:30 Coffee Break
10:30–11:00 Talk 22: TBA
11:00–11:30 Talk 23: TBA
11:30–13:30 Lunch Break
13:30–14:00 Talk 24: TBA
14:00–14:30 Talk 25: TBA
14:30–15:00 Coffee Break
15:00–15:30 Talk 26: TBA
15:30–16:00 Talk 27: TBA
16:00–16:15 Closing

Invited Talks

Invited talks will be scheduled throughout the workshop.

item.title: Registration
item.description:
Registration for TNSAA 2026 is now open.

Please complete the primary registration form below.
If you are unable to access Google Forms from your region or institution, please use the alternative registration form.

Primary Registration Form Alternative Registration Form

Please submit only one registration form. Do not submit both forms.

Some parts of the alternative form interface may appear in Chinese, but responses may be submitted in English.

item.title: Speakers
item.description:
Invited speakers will be announced soon.

Invited Speakers

TBA

To be announced

Invited speakers will be listed here.

Contributed Presentations

TNSAA 2026 will welcome contributed oral and poster presentations.

Submission details will be announced later.

item.title: Venue
item.description:
TNSAA 2026 will be held at the RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS) in Kobe, Japan.

RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS)

RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS) 7-1-26 Minatojima-minamimachi, Chuo-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 650-0047, Japan

R-CCS is one of Japan’s leading research centers for high-performance computing and computational science. It is located on Port Island in Kobe, a major international port city in western Japan.

For official access information, please refer to the RIKEN R-CCS directions page.
